machinery_genes.quote: All type III secretion systems (T3SSs) have a secretin, and one of these is the YsaC secretin of the chromosomally encoded Ysa T3SS of Yersinia enterocolitica.
machinery_genes.note: Rau & Darwin 2015 (J Bacteriol). YsaC = the Ysa secretin (SctC/InvG/MxiD/YscC family). The YspM paper (Witowski 2008, PMID 18805975) independently states 'YsaC is predicted to be the secretin for the Ysa T3SS based on homology to other T3SS secretins'.

machinery_genes.role: outer-membrane lipoprotein pilotin; required for YsaC secretin to localize to the outer membrane
machinery_genes.quote: YE3559, which we have named ysaP, is predicted to encode a small outer membrane lipoprotein ... All these observations suggest that YsaP is the pilotin for the YsaC secretin.
machinery_genes.note: Rau & Darwin 2015. Locus tag YE3559 given by the paper. IMPORTANT cross-check: the benchmark's curated effector list for this instance includes YE3559 as an effector, but the literature defines YE3559/YsaP as apparatus (the secretin pilotin), not a secreted effector. Flagged for the harness.

machinery_genes.quote: In Yersinia enterocolitica, YsaN was predicted as a putative ATPase of the Ysa-Ysp Type Three Secretion System (T3SS) based on sequence similarity with other T3S ATPases.
machinery_genes.note: Chatterjee, Halder & Datta 2013 (PLoS ONE); paper gives locus tag YsaN (YE3544) and shows purified YsaN is a Mg2+-dependent oligomeric ATPase. Homolog of YscN/InvC/Spa47/EscN (SctN). Further characterised biochemically in Kumar et al. 2022 (Front Mol Biosci, doi 10.3389/fmolb.2022.967974).

machinery_genes.quote: we created an in frame deletion of a gene that has homology to the hypothesized inner membrane pore, ysaV. The ysaV mutant strain failed to secrete eight proteins, called Ysps
machinery_genes.note: Haller, Carlson, Pederson & Pierson 2000 (Mol Microbiol), the founding paper for the chromosomal Ysa T3SS apparatus. YsaV is the SctV/InvA/MxiA/YscV-family export-apparatus inner-membrane component; its deletion abolishes Ysp secretion, confirming it is core apparatus.

machinery_genes.quote: The other mutants had transposon insertions that mapped to ysaI, ysaV, ysaW, and ysaT.
machinery_genes.note: Young & Young 2002 (J Bacteriol). YsaT named as a Ysa apparatus gene whose disruption blocks Ysa-dependent secretion. Assigned to the SctT/SctU export-gate family of the injectisome export apparatus by position in the ysa cluster and the apparatus brief vocabulary; exact Sct sub-assignment (T vs U) not stated in extractable text, so role is given at family-group resolution.

machinery_genes.quote: The other mutants had transposon insertions that mapped to ysaI, ysaV, ysaW, and ysaT.
machinery_genes.note: Young & Young 2002. YsaW named as a Ysa apparatus gene whose disruption blocks Ysa secretion. Exact Sct homolog not stated in extractable text; listed as apparatus on the strength of the secretion-defect phenotype.

machinery_genes.quote: The other mutants had transposon insertions that mapped to ysaI, ysaV, ysaW, and ysaT.
machinery_genes.note: Young & Young 2002. YsaI named as a Ysa apparatus gene whose disruption blocks Ysa secretion. Tentatively the SctI inner-rod homolog by name parallel (YscI/PrgJ/MxiI); exact Sct assignment not confirmed in extractable text.

machinery_genes.role: negative regulator of the YsaN ATPase (binds YsaN, inhibits its ATPase activity)
machinery_genes.quote: we have identified the existence of a negative regulator of YsaN--a hypothetical protein YE3555 (termed 'YsaL') ... YsaL inhibited the ATPase activity of YsaN
machinery_genes.note: Chatterjee et al. 2013. Locus tag YE3555. LOW-CONFIDENCE machinery call: YsaL directly partners and modulates the YsaN ATPase (so it is a cytoplasmic apparatus-associated component, analogous to the SctL ATPase stator/regulator), but the paper frames it as a regulator of the ATPase rather than a structural pore subunit. Included because it is part of the export-apparatus ATPase complex, not a transcriptional regulator and not a secreted substrate. Could be excluded if the benchmark counts only structural apparatus.

notes: Ysa = the chromosomally encoded second T3SS of Yersinia enterocolitica biovar 1B, found in strain 8081 (O:8), encoded in the YSA pathogenicity island / plasticity zone. It is distinct from (and acts independently of) the plasmid-borne Ysc-Yop injectisome and the flagellar T3SS. By sequence the Ysa apparatus is closest to the Shigella Mxi-Spa and Salmonella SPI-1 (Inv/Spa/Prg) injectisomes (Foultier 2002). The Thomson 2006 genome paper (PLoS Genet, open access) annotates the ysa operon as YE3533 [acpY] to YE3561 [ysrS]. WHY STATUS = PARTIAL: the full ~20-gene Sct apparatus roster (ysaB-ysaV with their individual Mxi-Spa/Sct homologs) is tabulated only in Foultier 2002 (J Mol Evol), which is paywalled, and in PMC full-text retrievals the italicised gene names are stripped to blanks, so I could not obtain verbatim per-gene naming quotes for most structural components. Following the hard anti-hallucination rule, I list only genes I can defend with an extractable verbatim quote: the secretin (YsaC), its pilotin (YsaP), the ATPase (YsaN) and its regulator (YsaL), the major export-apparatus ring (YsaV), and three further named apparatus genes whose disruption abolishes Ysa secretion (YsaI, YsaT, YsaW). The remaining structural genes (e.g. the SctJ/D inner rings, SctR/S/U export gate, SctF needle, SctQ C-ring) certainly exist in this operon but are not named in extractable text I could reach, so they are deliberately omitted rather than guessed. DELIBERATELY EXCLUDED as non-machinery: the secreted Ysps / effectors (YspA, YspB, YspC, YspD, YspM, plus the cross-secreted YopE), the chaperone SycB, and the transcriptional/regulatory genes YsaE (AraC-like activator), YsrR/YsrS (two-component system, YE3561=ysrS), and RcsCDB. NOTE the translocon proteins YspB/YspC form the host-membrane pore and are apparatus-like, but Foultier 2003 reports them as Ysa secretion SUBSTRATES (detected in culture supernatant), so they are left out of the machinery list here. DATA-QUALITY FLAG for the harness: the instance's curated effector list contains YE3559 and YE3561; YE3559 = ysaP (the secretin pilotin, apparatus, per Rau & Darwin 2015) and YE3561 = ysrS (a two-component sensor regulator), so at least these two 'effector' calls are mislabelled apparatus/regulator. Thomson 2006 PMID listed (17173484) is the genome annotation reference for the YE3533-YE3561 ysa operon coordinates; verify before relying on it for coordinate resolution.
